score_rationales.usability: Band 90+: one clearly primary action, conventional layout, visible active nav state, nothing destructive unguarded.
score_rationales.cognitive_load: Band 90+: two fields, one decision, no mental work the UI could do.
score_rationales.visual_layout: Band 90+: grouping matches semantics; single hierarchy passes the squint test.
score_rationales.accessibility: 88, not higher, only because a static image can't verify focus indicators or keyboard order — everything measurable passed (CTA 6.7:1, helper text 5.31:1, all inputs labeled).
score_rationales.content: 88: every visible string passes; a single screen can't confirm voice consistency across the rest of the product, which is what 90+ asserts.
score_rationales.trust_persuasion: 85: zero dark patterns; this screen simply has no high-stakes ask, so there is little for the category to positively demonstrate.

one_big_thing: Nothing to fix: this screen passes every static check that was run. The scores below 90+ reflect what a single static screenshot cannot prove (save feedback, focus states, cross-screen consistency), not hidden problems.
clean_checks: WCAG-3.3.2 — both inputs have persistent visible labels above the field
WCAG-1.4.3 — primary CTA white on #1d4ed8, measured 6.7:1 (needs 4.5:1)
WCAG-1.4.3 — helper text #636c79 on white, measured 5.31:1 (needs 4.5:1)
WCAG-1.4.1 — active nav item marked by weight + accent bar, not color alone
WCAG-2.5.8 — both buttons ~48px tall, double the 24px desktop minimum
NLS-02 — 'Full name', 'Email address', 'Save changes': plain user language, zero jargon
NLS-04 — header/sidebar/form layout follows settings-page convention
NLS-06 — current values shown in fields; nothing to remember or re-enter
NLS-08 — no promotional or irrelevant content competing with the task
NRM-02 — buttons look like buttons, fields like fields; no false affordances (RF-01)
NRM-06 — active nav state and enabled controls clearly distinguishable at a glance
GST-01/GST-07 — label, field, and helper text grouped tighter than unrelated items
GP-08 — only the two task-relevant fields on screen; no admin metadata
HICK-01/RF-08 — exactly one primary action; Cancel visibly secondary
CNT-01/RF-04 — 'Save changes' names its outcome, not its mechanism
CNT-08 — helper copy is one short purposeful sentence
CNT-10 — email field carries inline guidance ('only for receipts')
JAKOB-01 — matches the settings pattern of every familiar SaaS product
DARK-01..06 — no countdown, scarcity, pre-checked costs, confirmshaming, or hidden fees
Not statically auditable — save feedback (NLS-01), focus order, keyboard access: verify live
